name: Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón
region_intro: Mountain biking around A Fonsagrada offers a diverse landscape characterized by rugged mountainous terrain and dense forests in the central-eastern part of Lugo, Galicia. The region, part of the Oscos-Terra de Burón Biosphere Reserve, features significant elevation changes and numerous natural water features. Trails often traverse through varied countryside, including sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. This environment provides a challenging and scenic setting for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around A Fonsagrada The most popular mountain bike trail is Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo y Terras de Burón, a 40.1 miles (64.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the Río Eo and Terras de Burón Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alto das Penas desde Baleira — circular por la Reserva de Biosfera Río Eo, a moderate 18.3 miles (29.5 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop through the Río Eo Biosphere Reserve, providing varied terrain suitable for intermediate riders. Local mountain bikers also love the Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia, a 19.3 miles (31.1 km) trail leading through the Navia River area, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. This difficult route includes challenging ascents and descents. Mountain biking around A Fonsagrada is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and the Navia River valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging climbs. The routes in A Fonsagrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore A Fonsagrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around A Fonsagrada?
A: A Fonsagrada offers a diverse landscape characterized by rugged mountainous terrain, dense forests, and significant elevation changes. You'll find trails winding through mountains and forests, often including sections of the historic Camino Primitivo. The region is part of the Oscos-Terra de Burón Biosphere Reserve, providing a challenging and scenic environment with varied countryside.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels in A Fonsagrada?
A: Yes, the area provides options for various ability levels. While there are no easy trails listed, you'll find 7 moderate routes and 10 difficult routes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210479788/alto-das-penas-desde-baleira-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto das Penas desde Baleira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.3-mile (29.5 km) loop, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210343234/alto-de-pedras-apanadas-desde-fonsagrada-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.1-mile (64.5 km) trail with substantial elevation gain.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural sights to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada is known for its abundance of springs and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Murias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural monuments in the area. The region's name, derived from 'Fons Sacrata' (sacred fountain), highlights its many water features.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in A Fonsagrada?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in A Fonsagrada are designed as circular loops, offering a convenient way to explore the region. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210343234/alto-de-pedras-apanadas-desde-fonsagrada-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210479788/alto-das-penas-desde-baleira-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto das Penas desde Baleira&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are circular trails.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical and cultural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montouto Hospital and Dolmen&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical site. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4530231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Carmen de Vilabade&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz&lt;/a&gt;, offering insights into the region's heritage.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada is well-suited for demanding rides. Many routes feature significant elevation changes. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210654475/paradela-y-penamil-desde-a-pobra-circular-del-rio-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.3-mile (31.1 km) trail with challenging ascents and descents along the Navia River. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210394950/bustelin-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 36.5 km with over 1200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in A Fonsagrada?
A: The mountain bike routes in A Fonsagrada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. Over 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, scenic views, and the opportunity to ride through dense forests and along the Navia River.

Q: Do any mountain bike trails pass through the Oscos-Terra de Burón Biosphere Reserve?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada is part of the Oscos-Terra de Burón Biosphere Reserve, and several trails traverse this protected area. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210343234/alto-de-pedras-apanadas-desde-fonsagrada-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211504216/cascada-de-seimeira-de-murias-desde-pumares-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Seimeira de Murias desde Pumares&lt;/a&gt; both offer routes through the Río Eo and Terras de Burón Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Is it possible to mountain bike on sections of the Camino Primitivo near A Fonsagrada?
A: Yes, A Fonsagrada is integrated into the Camino Primitivo, one of the oldest routes of the Camino de Santiago. This historical trail utilizes dirt tracks, paths, and minor roads, offering challenging ascents and descents that are well-suited for mountain biking. Cyclists can follow these paths, which provide a unique blend of historical significance and rugged terrain.

Q: When is the best time to mountain bike in A Fonsagrada?
A: A Fonsagrada's lush countryside, part of 'España Verde,' suggests that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, avoiding the peak heat of summer. However, due to its mountainous nature, conditions can vary, so checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly mountain biking in A Fonsagrada?
A: While A Fonsagrada is known for its rugged and challenging terrain, there are moderate routes that might be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210479788/alto-das-penas-desde-baleira-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto das Penas desde Baleira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.3-mile (29.5 km) loop that offers varied terrain without the extreme difficulty of some other trails.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in A Fonsagrada?
A: A Fonsagrada's natural environment, including dense forests and mountainous terrain, generally welcomes dogs on trails. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions, especially within protected areas like the Oscos-Terra de Burón Biosphere Reserve, and to ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash when necessary.
